original: First truly complete sequence of a human genome published by Telomere-to-Telomere (T2T) consortium, after breakthroughs in new technology (previously just over 90% coded)
SpaceX mission Fram2 launches four people aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, becoming the first crewed spaceflight to enter a polar retrograde orbit
Explorer 1 re-enters the Earth's atmosphere after 12 years in orbit
Philadelphia Flyers score an NHL record tying 8 goals in the 2nd period of a 10-2 win over the visiting New York Islanders at the Spectrum
Tomohiro Nishikado, Japanese video gamer developer (Space Invaders), born in Osaka, Japan
Edwin Catmull. American computer scientist and film animator (co-founder of Pixar; President of Walt Disney Animation Studios, 2006-18), born in Parkersburg, West Virginia
Mike Bossybecomes first player in NHL history to record 7 straight 50 goal seasons; scores 50th and 51st of the year in a 3-1 New York Islanders' win at Washington
USSR launches Luna 10, 1st lunar orbiter
Dionisio Castro cycles world record 20km (57:18.4)
The Journal Nature reports the finding in Ethiopia of the first complete Australopithecus afarensis skull
Australian swimmer Lisbeth Lenton breaks Inge de Bruijn's 4-year old 100m freestyle women's world record in 53.66s at the Australian Olympic trials in Sydney
British pensioner Robert Weighton becomes the world's oldest man at 112 years
Jimi Hendrixfirst burns his guitar (and his fingers) at Finsbury Park Astoria theatre in London; the guitar is sold at auction for nearly $500K, the building is currently a church
Chicago White Sox assigns former NBA superstarMichael Jordanto the Birmingham Barons of Class AA Southern League; returns to NBA after one season

Eiffel Tower officially opens for dignitaries and an award ceremony in Paris, France; designed byGustave Eiffeland built for the Exposition Universelle, at 300 meters high, it holds the record for the tallest man-made structure for 41 years
Jean-Christophe Lafaille, French mountaineer best known for his solo self-rescue on Annapurna with a broken arm, born in Gap, Hautes-Alpes (d. 2006)
LA ends streetcar service after 90 years
USSR suspends nuclear weapons tests, & urges US & Britain to do same
Space Shuttle STS 76 (Atlantis 16), lands
Rodgers & Hammerstein's live television musical "Cinderella", starringJulie Andrews, with Kaye Ballard and Alice Ghostley, premieres on CBS-TV
American tennis starBillie Jean Kingends AustralianMargaret Court's 57-match winning streak 6-7, 7-6, 6-3 in semi finals of the WTA event in Indianapolis, IN
Boston Bruins defencemanBobby Orrscores his 3rd career hat trick in a 7-3 loss at Toronto to become the first player in NHL history to score 100 points for 4 straight seasons
German brothers Michael andRalf Schumacherbecome first siblings to share front row of the grid in a Formula 1 World Championship event; qualify 1st and 2nd respectively for Brazilian GP in São Paulo
(William) Lawrence Bragg, English physicist (Nobel Prize for Physics, 1915 - crystalline structure analysis by means of X-rays), born in Adelaide, Australia (d. 1971)[1]
Gerald Pearson, American physicist and inventor of the solar cell, born in Salem, Oregon (d. 1987)[1]
Lucille Bliss, American voice actress (Cinderella, The Secret of NIMH), born in New York City (d. 2012)
West Indies cricket all-rounder Collie Smith scores 104 in his Test debut in a 9 wicket 1st Test defeat to Australia in Kingston, Jamaica
Richard Pearse flies a monoplane several hundred yards in New Zealand
USSR launches Kosmos 1000 navigational satellite
Ranji Trophy Cricket Final, Wankhede Stadium, Bombay: Bombay (Sanjay Manjrekar 224 in 6/690) beats Punjab (Vikram Rathour 177 in 372) on 1st innings
In Sydney, Australia, 2.2 million people take part in the first Earth Hour.

William Grant Still's opera "Troubled Island" with libretto byLangston Hughesand Verna Arvey, premieres at the New York City Opera; 1st grand opera composed by an African American produced by a major company
"Carol & Company" starringCarol Burnettpremieres on NBC-TV
Archibald Scott Couper, Scottish chemist (advances in chemical theory including bonding of carbon atoms), born in Kirkintilloch, Scotland (d. 1892)
Dugald Clerk, Scottish engineer and inventor (1st 2-stroke motorcycle engine), born in Glasgow, Scotland (d. 1932)
Karl Bonhoeffer, German psychiatrist and neurologist (resisted Nazi's Aktion T4 extermination campaign), born in Neresheim, Württemberg (d. 1948)
Sergei Diaghilev, Russian arts patron and ballet impresario (founder of the Imperial Ballet and Ballets Russes), born in Gruzino Novgorod, Russia (d. 1929)
Pieter "Piet" Jongeling, Dutch politician (GPV) and author of children's books (Scout: The Secret of the Swamp), born in Broeksterwâld, Netherlands (d. 1985)
Shōichi Yokoi, Imperial Japanese Army sergeant, World War II (surrendered in 1972), born in Saori, Empire of Japan (d. 1997)
Don Barksdale, American basketball forward (Olympic gold 1948, NBA All-Star 1953), born in Oakland, CA (d. 1993)
Christopher [Ronald] Walken, American actor (The Deer Hunter, Brainstorm, Pulp Fiction), born in New York City
Dennis Kamakahi, Hawaiian Grammy Award-winning slack-key guitarist, born in Honolulu, Territory of Hawaii (d. 2014)

RCA Victor of Camden, New Jersey, introduces the 45 RPM record player and the 7-inch single, a vinyl disc more durable than the 78 RPM shellac; initial releases include: Eddy Arnold's "Texarkana Baby"; Spade Cooley's "Spanish Fandango"; Arthur Crudup's "Crudup's After Hours"; and Saul Meisel's "A Klein Melamedl"
Newfoundland becomes Canada's 10th province
Pirate Radio Station Pegaus (NZ) begins transmitting
Mudslides caused by heavy rains sweep through Mocoa, Colombia killing more than 200
First performance ofRobert Schumann's Symphony No. 1 in B-flat major
US Congress authorizes Civilian Conservation Corps
First election is held in Greece after World War II
Ranji Trophy Cricket Final, Feroz Shah Kotla, Delhi: Delhi (Ajay Sharma 175 in 532) beats Tamil Nadu (449) on 1st innings
First time in MLB history, the regular season opens in March with Seattle Mariners beating Chicago White Sox, 3-2 in 12 innings at the Kingdome, Seattle
"10 Things I Hate About You", a modernization ofShakespeare's The Taming of the Shrew, starringJulia StilesandHeath Ledger, is released
Etta Baker, American blues guitarist, born in Caldwell County, North Carolina (d. 2006)
William Daniels, American Emmy Award-winning stage and screen actor (St Elsewhere - "Dr. Craig"; 1776; Knight Rider; Boy Meets World)), born in Brooklyn, New York City
Dermot Morgan, Irish actor and comedian (Father Ted Crilly-Father Ted), born in Dublin, Ireland (d. 1998)
Patrick G Forrester, Army Lt Colonel and astronaut, born in El Paso, Texas
Ron Brown, American athlete (Olympic gold 4 x 100m 1984), born in Los Angeles, California
John Taylor, American NFL wide receiver (3-time Super Bowl winner, SF 49ers), born in Philadelphia, PA
"Soperton News" publishes 1st newspaper on pine pulp paper, in Soperton, Georgia
Nikolai Gogol [Nikolay], Ukrainian-born Russian novelist, humorist, and dramatist (Dead Souls, The Inspector-General), born in Sorochintsy, Poltava Governorate, Russian Empire (d. 1852)
